title: Z-score lookback truncated to 192 vs 252
description: KITE provides max ~252 daily bars. After 50 for SMA and 10 for slope only ~192 slopes available for z-score rolling window. Spec uses 252 slopes.
spec_behavior: 252-slope rolling window for z-score mean and std
kite_behavior: ~192-slope rolling window due to bar limit
impact: Z-scores computed on 24% less history. Rolling mean/std differ which shifts z-scores and changes quintile assignments. Expect 10-15% of trades to differ. This is structural not rounding.
recommendation: Document as known limitation. No fix unless KITE provides more historical bars. Compare z-score distributions on sample days to quantify divergence.

title: Quintile method rank-based vs pd.qcut
description: KITE uses int(rank * 5.0 / n) + 1 which differs from pd.qcut at bin boundaries. ~2 symbols per day assigned differently.
impact: ~2 symbols/day at Q4/Q5 boundary may be assigned differently. Over 1500 trading days = ~3000 symbol-days affected. Some will trigger or miss entry signals.
recommendation: Acceptable as known divergence. Log every Q5 assignment and compare sample days against reference to confirm only boundary cases not systematic shifts.

title: Stop fill at market near close vs exact stop_price
description: Spec fills stopped trades at exact stop_price. KITE sells at market via algo_sell at close-30s. If stock gaps through stop KITE fill could be far from stop_price.
spec_behavior: Fill at exact stop_price when daily low breaches it
kite_behavior: Sell at market at close-30 seconds
impact: Affects 22% of trades (~456 stops). If stock gaps down 5% through stop: spec credits stop_price (optimistic) KITE fills at actual market price at close. Even 1% avg difference across 456 trades at $500K-$2M = millions of P&L divergence. Most likely source of the $15M gap.
recommendation: Implement intraday stop check that sells immediately when price breaches stop_price OR document as fill model difference and expect stopped-trade P&L to diverge significantly.

title: Stop check uses md.L1.low at close-30s
description: Spec checks if day's LOW at any point was below stop. KITE checks md.L1.low at close-30s. Need to confirm md.L1.low is the true session low.
spec_behavior: Check daily low (the actual intraday low of the entire session)
impact: If md.L1.low is not the true session low (e.g. rolling window or recent low) then intraday stop breaches could be missed entirely.
recommendation: Confirm md.L1.low is the true session low from open to current time. Test with a known stop-out trade.

title: Days held counting verification
description: Entry day = day 1 and exit when days_held >= 21. Must confirm days_held increments exactly once per trading day not per bar or callback.
spec_behavior: Entry day = day 1. Exit at close of day 20 (days_held = 21).
kite_behavior: days_held_init = 1 and increments in on_start daily
impact: If days_held increments more than once per day trades exit early. If it misses a day trades hold too long.
recommendation: Verify with single trade that Monday entry exits exactly 4 weeks later (20 trading days). Check days_held is not double-incremented on volatile days with multiple callbacks.

title: Entry fill at open+2s vs exact open price
description: Spec uses exact Polygon open. KITE fills at md.L1.last 2 seconds after open. On illiquid names ($3-$10 stocks) could be 5-10 cents different (2-3% of price).
impact: Small per-trade noise. On liquid names 1-2 cents. On illiquid names (JOBY IONQ PLUG) could be 5-10 cents. Adds noise across 2000+ trades but not systematic bias.
recommendation: Acceptable. Log every entry fill price and compare sample to Polygon opens to confirm no systematic bias.

title: Exit fill at close-30s vs exact close
description: Spec uses exact Polygon daily close. KITE fills at market 30 seconds before close. Can differ 10-50 bps on volatile days.
kite_behavior: Market fill at close minus 30 seconds
impact: Same noise issue as entry. Actually more realistic for live trading but prevents penny-for-penny matching.
recommendation: Acceptable. This is how real execution would work. Log fills and compare sample.

title: Cost model not applied in KITE
description: Spec deducts 5 bps each way (10 bps round trip) explicitly. KITE says P&L reflects actual fills with no additional cost deduction.
spec_behavior: Explicit 5 bps entry cost + 5 bps exit cost deducted from gross P&L
kite_behavior: Costs embedded in fill prices — no explicit deduction
impact: Raw P&L numbers not directly comparable. On 2082 trades at ~$1M avg notional 10 bps round trip = ~$2M total cost difference.
recommendation: Either add explicit cost tracking in KITE or compare GROSS P&L only. Document which comparison is being made.

title: No stock split handling plan
description: AAPL 4:1 Aug 2020 / TSLA 3:1 Aug 2022 / GOOGL 20:1 Jul 2022 / AMZN 20:1 Jun 2022 / SHOP 10:1 Jun 2022. With unadjusted prices the SMA50 has a discontinuity on split day.
spec_behavior: Split-adjusted data — SMA50 is continuous across splits
kite_behavior: Unadjusted prices — SMA50 jumps/drops by split ratio on split day
impact: An unadjusted SMA50 spanning a split date is garbage. GOOGL split 20:1 in Jul 2022 — SMA50 would jump from ~$2500 to ~$125 mid-window. Slope and z-score become meaningless for weeks after.
recommendation: MUST address. Either: (a) use adjusted data (b) reset SMA on split dates and recompute from adjusted history (c) apply split ratio to pre-split bars before computing SMA. This is not optional.

title: First ATR value computation not verified
description: Row 40 says mean(TR[1]...TR[14]) for the initial ATR seed. This matches spec. But if KITE's ATR implementation uses a different seed the entire ATR series diverges permanently.
spec_behavior: First ATR = simple mean of first 14 True Range values then Wilder's EMA from bar 15 onward
kite_behavior: States mean(TR[1]...TR[14]) but not verified in code
impact: If seed differs all subsequent ATR values differ which means all stop prices differ. ATR is a recursive formula — initial conditions matter forever.
recommendation: Verify ATR on a known symbol (e.g. MRK signal_date 2020-01-06 ATR should be 1.053433). If it matches to 4 decimal places the implementation is correct.

title: Body_pct boundaries not validated against KITE data
description: Fixed boundaries (-0.53 / -0.17 / +0.23 / +0.57) computed from Polygon data. Body_pct is a ratio so should be split-neutral but vendor rounding may cause differences.
spec_behavior: body_pct boundaries from Polygon split-adjusted OHLC
kite_behavior: Same boundaries applied to KITE unadjusted OHLC
impact: Body_pct = (C-O)/(H-L) should be identical for adjusted vs unadjusted since the split multiplier cancels. But if OHLC values have different decimal precision the ratio can shift at boundaries.
recommendation: Compute body_pct for 10 known trades in KITE and compare to our values. If they match to 2 decimal places the boundaries are safe. Focus on trades near boundaries (body_pct near -0.53 or +0.57).

title: No handling specified for halted/missing trading days
description: If a symbol has no bar on a given day what happens? Is it excluded from quintile sort? Does it carry forward previous z-score? What about the hold period counter?
spec_behavior: If date is missing in enriched CSV symbol is excluded from quintile sort that day. Hold period counts trading days with data.
impact: Missing days affect: (a) quintile sort composition (b) SMA50 continuity (c) hold period counting. If KITE carries forward stale data the z-score is wrong. If it skips the day the hold period may be miscounted.
recommendation: Define behavior for: (a) symbol with no bar today — exclude from quintile sort (b) hold period — count only days the symbol actually traded (c) SMA50 — do not include gaps use only actual trading days.
